query_normalized: predicting glass transition temperature from compositional descriptors with explainable boosting machines materials science
rationale: This paper addresses the fundamental thermodynamic mechanisms and variables (glass transition temperature $T_g$, specific heat jumps, enthalpy, and entropy) that define the target property the user aims to predict, satisfying criteria (a) for same-mechanism evidence and (b) for measuring the dependent variable on the same domain.
